description: Solo-Claude role manifest. builder = claude (this session). reviewer = user (human) or optional claude-reviewer (isolated session). No Codex path.

dispatch.note: Reviewers are NOT auto-invoked. Post-hook records a `review-needed` event; the user (or a separately launched isolated Claude Code session acting as `claude-reviewer`) must open the referenced sealed prompt, write `.claude/reviews/<ts>.md`, then record `02 pass <actor>` via scripts/harness/event_log.py. Gate ② P2-F enforces author != reviewer at verdict-record time, not at dispatch time.
dispatch.claude_reviewer_invocation: To run a claude-reviewer: open a new Claude Code session in the same repo with `CLAUDE_ACTOR=claude-reviewer` (informational only — actor is recorded by whoever calls event_log.py). The reviewer session should start by reading the sealed prompt referenced in the latest `review-needed` event's `suggested_sealed_prompt`, then nothing else from this author session.

roles.note: Claude drafts. Review happens in plan-redteam role below.
User reads the plan fresh. Alternatively, an isolated Claude session reviews without shared context.

actor_values.claude: primary author session (this Claude Code session)
actor_values.user: human — reads diffs/plans independently and records verdict
actor_values.claude-reviewer: optional isolated Claude session for fresh-eye review
actor_values.bash: synthesized by bash_write_probe for Bash-tool writes

actor_crossover_rule: Gate ② P2-F rejects self-review: review-needed.actor must differ from the actor that records `02 pass`. In solo mode, typical pairs are (claude→user), (claude→claude-reviewer).
